producer.name: Domaine Sigalas
producer.philosophy: Pioneer of modern Santorini winemaking. Paris Sigalas, a former mathematics professor, began bottling estate wines in 1991. Santorini's volcanic soils (pumice, ash, and lava over limestone), extreme Aegean winds, and intense sunlight produce some of the most mineral-driven white wines in the world. Vines are trained in the traditional kouloura (basket) system, low to the ground to protect from wind. Many vines are ungrafted pre-phylloxera stock, over 200 years old.

metadata.soil: Volcanic — pumice, ash, lava over limestone

wines.name: Santorini Assyrtiko
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Assyrtiko from old bush vines (kouloura trained) on volcanic soil. Stainless steel fermented to preserve minerality and acidity. No malolactic. Intense salinity and citrus with volcanic mineral character.

wines.name: Santorini Barrel
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Assyrtiko from old vines. Fermented and aged 6 months in French oak barrels. Oak adds textural complexity while preserving the volcanic mineral core. Partial malolactic.

wines.name: Kavalieros
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Assyrtiko from the Kavalieros vineyard — one of the highest sites on Santorini with 100+ year old vines on pure volcanic ash. Wild fermented in old French oak. Extended lees contact. The most concentrated and age-worthy Sigalas wine.

wines.name: Nykteri
wines.vinification_notes: Traditional Santorini designation: grapes harvested at high ripeness, pressed at night ('nykteri' = 'of the night'). Primarily Assyrtiko with Aidani and Athiri. Fermented and aged in French oak. Richer and more opulent than standard Assyrtiko.

wines.name: Vinsanto
wines.vinification_notes: Traditional Santorini dessert wine. Assyrtiko, Aidani, and Athiri grapes sun-dried for 10-14 days on rooftops, concentrating sugars. Barrel aged 2+ years. Amber color, intense dried fruit, honey, and spice. One of Greece's most ancient and storied wine styles.

wines.name: Mm (Mavrotragano)
wines.style: Rare indigenous red from volcanic terroir
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Mavrotragano — one of Greece's rarest indigenous red varieties, nearly extinct before revival on Santorini. Deep color, firm tannins, black fruit, and volcanic mineral character. Aged in French oak.
